title: Maps, Strings, Bytes, Runes, Unicode, and Deterministic Results · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident summarizer must count labels, normalize text, preserve UTF-8, and publish stable output despite unordered maps. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Unicode-safe deterministic incident summarizer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Create, update, delete, clear, and query maps while distinguishing missing keys from stored zero values.
Choose comparable key types and explain map reference behavior, iteration instability, mutation rules, and concurrency limits.
Treat strings as immutable byte sequences and convert to or from byte slices with explicit allocation and encoding intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
